page: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an x86_64 server belonging to the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz, delivering 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration consists of 512.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2097 GB of capacity and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip and bzip3 compression, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform significantly better. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and large-block uncached memory bandwidth, both read and write, rank in the top 25% tier. General multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations, consistently register in the middle 50% tier.

The server exhibits weak performance in single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation, making it poorly suited for latency-sensitive single-core applications or modern AI inference. However, its high memory capacity, strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth, and 2097 GB of local storage make it highly capable for memory-intensive, parallelized workloads. It is best suited for database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that can leverage its 64 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an x86_64 server in the MS family powered by a dedicated 2.7 GHz Intel Xeon 8280M processor. It provides 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores, 512.0 GB of RAM, and 2097 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage. While single-core performance and single-threaded compression benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%, the server excels in multi-threaded operations. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rank in the top 25%, while multi-core CPU benchmarks and database operations consistently perform in the middle 50% tier. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive, parallelized enterprise workloads rather than single-threaded or AI inference tasks. It is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an x86_64 server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2097 GB local storage. It delivers strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth, making it ideal for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64ls is an x86_64 server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2097 GB storage, optimized for multi-threaded database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 platform with high memory capacity designed for database operations, large scale data processing, and parallel enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
512 GB of system memory providing 8 GB per core for memory-intensive tasks
2097 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth and decompression performance
Suited for database hosting, software compilation, and parallel data processing
Lower performance in single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation
